Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Digestive.name: Gracilaria Digestive Kashaya
preparation_steps: Wash the dried Gracilaria thoroughly and soak it for about 5 minutes to soften.
Add the soaked seaweed, Triphala, and water into a pan.
Heat the mixture and allow it to boil for around 10 minutes.
Reduce the flame and let it simmer gently for another 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain the liquid to separate the herbs.
Serve the warm Kashaya immediately for best results.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Skin Paste.name: Gracilaria Skin Paste (Lepa)
preparation_steps: Place the Gracilaria gel and Aloe vera gel in a clean mixing bowl.
Mix both gels slowly until an even smooth texture forms.
Add rose water little by little while stirring.
Continue mixing for about 5 minutes to ensure full blending.
Apply directly on skin or refrigerate for later use.
Use within 24 hours for best freshness.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Skin Paste.contraindications: Do patch test for sensitive skin

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Hair Oil (.name: Gracilaria Hair Oil (Thailaya)
preparation_steps: Warm the sesame oil gently until it reaches a mild heat (not too hot).
Add the Gracilaria extract into the oil while stirring slowly.
Add Bhringraj extract and continue stirring for around 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool naturally.
Filter if needed to remove residues.
Pour the oil into a dry bottle for storage.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Mineral Dr.name: Gracilaria Mineral Drink (Panaka)
preparation_steps: Heat the water to about 50-60°C.
Add Gracilaria powder and stir well to dissolve.
Add jaggery and mix until fully melted.
Add lemon juice and stir for another 1-2 minutes.
Filter the drink to remove any particles.
Serve warm for better absorption.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Digestive.name: Gracilaria Digestive Powder(Churna)
preparation_steps: Dry the Gracilaria at 50°C until it becomes crisp.
Grind the dried seaweed into a fine powder.
Mix the seaweed powder and dry ginger powder thoroughly.
Pass the mixture through a sieve to ensure uniformity.
Place the powder in an air-tight, moisture-free container.
Store in a cool dry place.

Gracilaria Edulis||Anti-inflammatory Lep.name: Anti-inflammatory Lepa
preparation_steps: Add Gracilaria gel and turmeric paste into a bowl.
Mix both ingredients until a smooth yellow paste is formed.
Add sesame oil slowly to improve spreadability.
Continue stirring for 5-7 minutes.
Apply directly to the swollen area.
Leave for 20-30 minutes before washing.

Gracilaria Edulis||Skin Hydration Drink.name: Skin Hydration Drink
preparation_steps: Warm the water to about 50°C.
Add the Gracilaria powder and mix well until dissolved.
Add honey and stir until evenly blended.
Let the drink settle for 1 minute.
Filter if desired.
Drink while warm.

Gracilaria Edulis||Rejuvenation Churna.name: Rejuvenation Churna
preparation_steps: Measure Gracilaria powder and Ashwagandha powder in equal proportions.
Mix both powders thoroughly in a dry bowl.
Sieve the combined powder to ensure even particle size.
Transfer the mixture into a container.
Ensure the lid is tightly sealed to avoid moisture absorption.
Label with date and batch number.
Gracilaria Edulis||Rejuvenation Churna.contraindications: Avoid high dose if digestive sensitivity

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Facial Mas.name: Gracilaria Facial Mask
preparation_steps: Add Gracilaria gel into a clean bowl.
Add sandalwood paste and mix well.
Stir until a soft, spreadable paste forms.
Apply evenly on the face using clean fingers.
Leave it for 10-15 minutes.
Rinse with lukewarm water.

Gracilaria Edulis||Hair Strengthening Oi.name: Hair Strengthening Oil
preparation_steps: Warm the coconut oil lightly (not boiling).
Add Gracilaria extract slowly while stirring.
Continue mixing for 10 minutes.
Allow the oil to cool completely.
Filter if any residue remains.
Store in a sterilized bottle.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gut Tonic Drink.name: Gut Tonic Drink
preparation_steps: Boil water for about 5 minutes.
Add Gracilaria powder and ginger slices into the boiling water.
Stir for around 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and cover for 2 minutes.
Strain the mixture carefully.
Drink warm.

Gracilaria Edulis||Prebiotic Powder.name: Prebiotic Powder
preparation_steps: Dry the seaweed completely at 50°C to remove moisture.
Grind the dried Gracilaria into a fine, smooth powder.
Add fenugreek powder and mix thoroughly.
Sieve the powder mixture to get uniform texture.
Store in a dry, clean, air-tight container.
Keep away from sunlight.
Gracilaria Edulis||Prebiotic Powder.contraindications: Avoid high fiber if digestive issues

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Energy Gel.name: Gracilaria Energy Gel
preparation_steps: Warm water to 45-50°C.
Add honey and stir until fully dissolved.
Slowly sprinkle Gracilaria powder while continuously stirring to avoid clumps.
Continue mixing until a uniform gel begins to form.
Add lemon juice and mix gently.
Cool the gel to room temperature and transfer to airtight containers.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Energy Gel.contraindications: Avoid if allergic to honey or seaweed

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Detox Tea.name: Gracilaria Detox Tea
preparation_steps: Heat water to about 90°C (not boiling).
Add dried Gracilaria and ginger slices.
Cover the cup/pot and let steep for 10 minutes.
Stir once and strain through a fine mesh filter.
Serve hot.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Detox Tea.contraindications: Not for people with iodine sensitivity

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Soup Mix.name: Gracilaria Soup Mix
preparation_steps: Dry Gracilaria flakes in a dehydrator at 55-60°C until crisp.
Grind carrot powder and other ingredients to the same particle size.
Add all powdered ingredients into a blender or tumbler mixer.
Mix for 5-7 minutes until fully uniform.
Package immediately in moisture-proof bags.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Skin Hydra.name: Gracilaria Skin Hydrating Gel
preparation_steps: Prepare aloe gel and distilled water in a sanitized vessel.
Add Gracilaria extract slowly while stirring.
Add glycerin.
Homogenize using a high-shear mixer for 10-15 minutes.
Adjust pH to 5.5-6 using citric acid if needed.
Filter (optional) and fill into sterilized containers.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Smoothie B.name: Gracilaria Smoothie Base
preparation_steps: Wash banana and peel.
Add Gracilaria slurry, banana, and coconut water into blender.
Blend at high speed for 30-45 seconds until smooth.
Chill immediately to retain freshness.
Fill into bottles if storing short-term.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Smoothie B.contraindications:
Avoid for people with seaweed/iodine allergy. 
Diabetics monitor natural sugar intake. 
Consume fresh to prevent microbial growth.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Immune Pow.name: Gracilaria Immune Powder
preparation_steps: Dry Gracilaria in an oven at 55-60°C until moisture is below 8%.
Mill into fine powder.
Sieve through 80-mesh screen.
Mix with moringa and cinnamon in a dry tumbler for 5-10 minutes.
Pack into airtight pouches.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Immune Pow.contraindications:
Not recommended for thyroid disorders without medical advice (iodine content).
Pregnant users consult doctor.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Immune Pow.recommended_dose: 5-10 g/day mixed with water, juice, or smoothie

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Calcium Po.name: Gracilaria Calcium Porridge Mix
preparation_steps: Mill Gracilaria into fine flour.
Combine rice flour, milk powder, and salt in a mixing bowl.
Stir for 5 minutes until uniform.
Store in airtight containers.
For use: Add water/milk and cook while stirring until thick.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Calcium Po.contraindications:
Lactose-intolerant individuals should replace milk powder. 
Avoid excess intake with calcium supplements.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Green Juic.name: Gracilaria Green Juice
preparation_steps: Add spinach, seaweed juice, lime, and water into blender.
Blend at high speed for 60 seconds.
Strain the juice using a muslin cloth or fine sieve.
Serve chilled.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Green Juic.contraindications:
High oxalate (spinach)  kidney stone patients limit intake. 
Consume immediately after preparation.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Green Juic.recommended_dose: 150-200 ml per serving, max once daily

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Hair Nouri.name: Gracilaria Hair Nourish Mask
preparation_steps: Blend curry leaves with small amount of water to paste.
Add seaweed gel and coconut milk into bowl.
Mix using a spatula until smooth.
Use immediately or refrigerate for up to 24 hours.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Hair Nouri.contraindications:
External use only. 
Patch test recommended to avoid scalp irritation. 
Use within 24 hrs refrigerated.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Face Mask.name: Gracilaria Face Mask Powder
preparation_steps: Dry ingredients completely.
Sieve all powders through 80-100 mesh.
Use a dry mixer to combine for 5-7 minutes.
Store in a dry jar.
To use: mix 1 tbsp with 2-3 tsp water to make paste.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Face Mask.contraindications:
Avoid on broken or sensitive skin. 
Patch test required due to clay absorption effect.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Fiber Caps.name: Gracilaria Fiber Capsules
preparation_steps: Dry and mill Gracilaria fiber into a fine powder.
Sieve through 100-mesh.
Load powder into capsule-filling machine.
Fill 500 mg per capsule.
Inspect for weight variation.
Seal and pack.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Fiber Caps.contraindications:
May cause bloating if taken without enough water. 
Avoid intestinal obstruction patients.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Fiber Caps.recommended_dose: 1-2 capsules (500 mg each), twice daily with water

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Yogurt Add.name: Gracilaria Yogurt Additive (probiotic support)
preparation_steps: Prepare yogurt and chill to 4°C.
Add seaweed gel at 10 ml per 100 g yogurt.
Mix gently using spatula to avoid breaking curd.
Store refrigerated.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Yogurt Add.contraindications:
Maintain cold chain to protect probiotics. 
Not for lactose intolerance unless dairy-free yogurt used.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Yogurt Add.recommended_dose: 10 g gel per 100 g yogurt serving

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Protein Sn.name: Gracilaria Protein Snack Bar
preparation_steps: Roast oats and peanuts lightly.
Heat honey until slightly thin.
Add Gracilaria flakes and mix thoroughly.
Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
Press mixture into baking tray.
Bake lightly (optional) or refrigerate until set.
Cut into bars.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Protein Sn.contraindications:
Contains peanuts (allergen warning). 
High calorie  portion control needed.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Digestive.name: Gracilaria Digestive Tonic
preparation_steps: Boil water with cumin for 2 minutes.
Add Gracilaria and turn off heat.
Cover and let steep for 5 minutes.
Strain and serve warm.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Digestive.contraindications:
Avoid excessive intake in gastritis or acid-sensitive individuals.
Serve warm only.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Electrolyt.name: Gracilaria Electrolyte Drink
preparation_steps: Dissolve sugar and salt in water.
Add seaweed mineral extract.
Mix using stirrer for 2-3 minutes.
Chill and bottle.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Electrolyt.contraindications:
Monitor sodium intake for hypertension patients. 
Not a replacement for medical ORS in severe dehydration.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Hair Serum.name: Gracilaria Hair Serum (oil-based)
preparation_steps: Warm sesame oil to 35-40°C.
Add seaweed extract and stir gently.
Add vitamin E and mix again.
Let cool and bottle.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Hair Serum.contraindications:
External use only. 
Avoid contact with eyes. 
Store away from heat to prevent oil rancidity.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Skin Toner.name: Gracilaria Skin Toner
preparation_steps: Filter seaweed extract to make it clear.
Combine rose water in sterile tank.
Add seaweed filtrate slowly with gentle stirring.
Add preservative.
Adjust pH to 4.5-5.5.
Fill into spray bottles.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Skin Toner.contraindications:
Patch test required. 
Avoid use on irritated skin. 
Maintain preservative stability.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Bath Salt.name: Gracilaria Bath Salt Blend
preparation_steps: Dry seaweed granules to reduce moisture.
Add Epsom salt to mixer.
Add essential oil dropwise while mixing.
Add dried seaweed granules.
Blend until uniform.
Pack into jars.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Bath Salt.contraindications:
Avoid use on open wounds or very sensitive skin. 
Essential oils may irritate some users.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Relax Tea.name: Gracilaria Relax Tea Blend
preparation_steps: Dry Gracilaria, chamomile, and lemongrass.
Cut/sieve to uniform size.
Mix thoroughly in dry mixing drum.
Pack in tea bags or jars.
For use: steep 8-10 minutes in 90°C water.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Relax Tea.contraindications:
Chamomile allergy possible (ragweed-sensitive individuals). 
Avoid excessive intake during pregnancy without advice.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Relax Tea.recommended_dose: 1 tea bag / 3-5 g per cup, up to 2 cups/day

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Rehydratio.name: Gracilaria Rehydration Soup
preparation_steps: Boil vegetable broth.
Add seaweed flakes.
Simmer for 3-5 minutes.
Stir gently to rehydrate evenly.
Season and serve.
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Rehydratio.contraindications:
High sodium if additional salt added  monitor for hypertension patients. 
Consume fresh.

Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Prebiotic.name: Gracilaria Prebiotic Powder Pack
preparation_steps: Prepare oligosaccharide-rich seaweed extract.
Add maltodextrin as a carrier.
Pre-mix using a high-shear stirrer.
Spray dry mixture at controlled temperature.
Collect dried powder.
Sieve and pack into sachets.
critical_control_points: Spray-dryer parameters (inlet/outlet temp, feed rate) critical
Gracilaria Edulis||Gracilaria Prebiotic.contraindications:
May cause mild gas during gut adaptation phase. 
Start with small doses.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Hydrating Kashaya.name: Hydrating Kashaya
preparation_steps: Wash the Kappaphycus thoroughly and soak it for 5 minutes to remove excess salt and soften.
Add the seaweed, ginger, cinnamon, and water into a pot.
Heat the mixture and boil for about 10 minutes.
Lower the heat and simmer gently for another 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove solid herbs.
Drink warm for better digestion and hydration.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Skin Paste (Lepa).name: Skin Paste (Lepa)
preparation_steps: Add Kappaphycus gel and Aloe vera gel into a clean bowl.
Mix the gels slowly until they combine into a smooth, even texture.
Add rose water gradually while stirring.
Continue mixing for about 5 minutes for a uniform blend.
Apply directly to the skin or store in a refrigerator.
Use within 24 hours for maximum freshness.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Hair Oil (Thailay.name: Hair Oil (Thailaya)
preparation_steps: Warm the sesame oil gently until it becomes mildly heated (not too hot).
Slowly add Kappaphycus extract into the warm oil while stirring.
Add Bhringraj extract and continue stirring for around 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and allow the oil to cool fully.
Filter to remove any residue if needed.
Store the prepared oil in a dry glass bottle.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Mineral Drink (Pa.name: Mineral Drink (Panaka)
preparation_steps: Heat water to around 50-60°C.
Add Kappaphycus powder and stir well until fully dissolved.
Add jaggery and mix until it melts completely.
Add lemon juice and stir for 1-2 minutes.
Filter the drink to remove undissolved particles.
Serve warm or mildly hot.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Digestive Powder.name: Digestive Powder (Churna)
preparation_steps: Dry the seaweed at 50°C until fully crisp.
Grind the dried Kappaphycus into a fine powder.
Add dry ginger powder and mix thoroughly.
Sieve the powder for even particle size.
Store in an airtight moisture-free container.
Keep in a cool and dry environment.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Anti-inflammatory.name: Anti-inflammatory Lepa
preparation_steps: Add Kappaphycus gel and turmeric paste into a bowl.
Mix both ingredients until they form a smooth, even paste.
Add sesame oil gradually to improve application smoothness.
Stir for 5-7 minutes to ensure proper blending.
Apply evenly on the affected swollen area.
Leave for 20-30 minutes before washing off.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Skin Hydration Dr.name: Skin Hydration Drink
preparation_steps: Warm water to about 50°C.
Add Kappaphycus powder and stir until dissolved.
Add honey and continue mixing.
Allow the drink to settle for a minute.
Filter if necessary.
Drink while warm.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Hydrating Jelly.name: Hydrating Jelly
preparation_steps: Heat fruit juice to 60-70°C.
Dissolve sugar fully while stirring.
Add Kappaphycus gel slowly and mix until evenly blended.
Pour the mixture into molds while still warm.
Allow it to cool for 1-2 hours until jelly sets.
Store in a refrigerator for better firmness.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappaphycus Hydra.name: Kappaphycus Hydrating Herbal Drink
preparation_steps: Measure 10 g Kappaphycus powder.
Heat 150 ml water to 50-60°C.
Add powder to warm water and stir 2-3 min until fully hydrated.
Add honey and mix until dissolved.
Add lime juice and stir gently.
Serve immediately or store chilled.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappaphycus Hydra.contraindications:
Avoid for iodine/seaweed allergy. 
Diabetics monitor honey intake. 
Consume fresh to avoid contamination.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Mineral S.name: Seaweed Mineral Soup
preparation_steps: Wash and chop vegetables.
Boil 250 ml water in pot.
Add chopped vegetables and simmer 10 min.
Add Kappaphycus and simmer 5-7 min.
Season to taste.
Serve hot.
critical_control_points: Avoid overcooking seaweed to prevent nutrient loss
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Mineral S.contraindications:
High mineral content  kidney patients consult doctor. 
Avoid excessive salt addition.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Smoothi.name: Red Algae Smoothie Mix
preparation_steps: Peel and slice banana.
Measure 8 g seaweed powder.
Add banana, seaweed powder, yogurt, and honey into blender.
Blend for 1-2 min until smooth.
Check consistency and adjust with water if needed.
Serve chilled.
critical_control_points: Avoid prolonged blending that may oxidize nutrients
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Smoothi.contraindications:
Not suitable for lactose intolerance (if yogurt used). 
Refrigerate immediately after preparation.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Energy Ge.name: Seaweed Energy Gel
preparation_steps: Measure Kappaphycus gel 20 g.
Add 30 g glucose to gel and stir well.
Incorporate fruit puree and mix thoroughly.
Check uniformity of mixture.
Fill into containers.
Store in cool, dry place.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Energy Ge.contraindications:
High glucose  diabetics use cautiously. 
Store cool to prevent spoilage.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Fiber Cap.name: Seaweed Fiber Capsules
preparation_steps: Dry Kappaphycus thoroughly.
Grind to fine powder.
Sieve to ensure uniform particle size.
Fill powder into capsules using capsule filler.
Seal and label capsules.
Store in dry, cool conditions.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Fiber Cap.contraindications:
Drink sufficient water to avoid constipation. 
Avoid intestinal obstruction patients.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappa Skin Hydrat.name: Kappa Skin Hydration Mask
preparation_steps: Measure Kappaphycus and aloe gels.
Blend Kappaphycus gel with aloe gel for 2-3 min.
Add rose water gradually while stirring.
Homogenize mixture until uniform.
Check pH and viscosity.
Fill into sterile jar and store cold.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappa Skin Hydrat.contraindications:
External use only. 
Patch test required. 
Avoid broken skin.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Body Scru.name: Seaweed Body Scrub
preparation_steps: Melt coconut oil if solid.
Mix sugar into oil.
Gradually add seaweed powder.
Stir until homogeneous.
Check texture; adjust if needed.
Package in container.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Body Scru.contraindications:
Avoid sensitive or wounded skin. 
Oil may clog acne-prone skin.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Body Scru.recommended_dose: Use small amount during bath, 2-3 times/week

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappa Hair Growth.name: Kappa Hair Growth Serum
preparation_steps: Warm coconut oil to 35-40°C.
Add Kappaphycus extract and stir 3 min.
Incorporate vitamin E.
Homogenize mixture.
Cool to room temperature.
Fill into amber bottles.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappa Hair Growth.contraindications:
External use only. 
Avoid eye contact. 
Stop use if irritation occurs.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Mineral Electroly.name: Mineral Electrolyte Powder
preparation_steps: Measure ingredients accurately.
Dry blend powders in mixer 5 min.
Sieve to remove lumps.
Pack into sachets.
Seal airtight.
Label and store dry.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Mineral Electroly.contraindications:
Monitor sodium intake for hypertension patients. 
Not medical ORS replacement.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Herbal Te.name: Seaweed Herbal Tea
preparation_steps: Place flakes and lemongrass into teapot.
Boil water to 90°C.
Pour water over herbs.
Steep 5-7 min.
Strain tea.
Serve hot.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Herbal Te.contraindications:
Possible herbal allergy (lemongrass sensitive individuals). 
Avoid excessive intake during pregnancy.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Protein.name: Red Algae Protein Balls
preparation_steps: Roast oats lightly for 5 minutes at 100°C.
Mix Kappaphycus powder and nuts in a bowl.
Warm honey slightly and add to the dry mix.
Stir to form a sticky dough.
Roll into small balls and chill 30 min before serving.
critical_control_points: Ensure even mixing of powder and honey
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Protein.contraindications:
Contains nuts (allergen warning). 
High calorie snack.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Detox Dri.name: Seaweed Detox Drink
preparation_steps: Boil water to 80°C.
Add ginger and steep 5 min.
Add Kappaphycus powder and stir until dissolved.
Add lemon juice and mix gently.
Serve warm or chill for later consumption.
critical_control_points: Fresh water and lemon for flavor and hygiene
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Detox Dri.contraindications:
Acidic due to lemon  gastritis patients limit intake. 
Consume fresh.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Sea Moss Jelly.name: Sea Moss Jelly
preparation_steps: Heat fruit juice to 60-70°C.
Add sugar and stir until dissolved.
Gradually add Kappaphycus gel while stirring.
Pour mixture into molds.
Cool at 10-15°C until set.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Sea Moss Jelly.contraindications:
High sugar if added  diabetics monitor intake. 
Refrigerate after preparation.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Facial.name: Red Algae Facial Toner
preparation_steps: Filter Kappaphycus extract to remove solids.
Mix extract with distilled water at room temperature.
Homogenize gently for 5 min.
Adjust pH to 5.5-6 using citric acid if needed.
Bottle in sterilized containers.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Facial.contraindications:
External use only. 
Patch test required. 
Maintain sterility.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Mineral Broth Pow.name: Mineral Broth Powder
preparation_steps: Dry Kappaphycus powder at 45-55°C until moisture <10%.
Grind into fine powder (200-300 microns).
Sieve to ensure uniform particle size.
Tumble blend to ensure homogeneity.
Pack into air-tight moisture-proof containers.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Mineral Broth Pow.contraindications:
Avoid excessive mineral intake alongside supplements. 
Store dry.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Mineral Broth Pow.recommended_dose: 5-10 g per serving mixed with hot water

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Instant S.name: Seaweed Instant Soup Sachet
preparation_steps: Grind Kappaphycus and vegetable powders finely.
Mix all dry ingredients in a clean bowl.
Sieve to remove clumps and ensure uniformity.
Tumble blend for 5-10 minutes.
Pack into air-tight, moisture-proof sachets.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Instant S.contraindications: Contains salt  hypertension patients limit intake.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappa Hydration J.name: Kappa Hydration Jelly Cubes
preparation_steps: Heat fruit juice to 60-70°C.
Dissolve sugar in the warm juice.
Gradually add Kappaphycus gel while stirring gently.
Pour mixture into molds.
Cool at 10-15°C until gel sets.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappa Hydration J.contraindications:
High sugar variants should be limited for diabetics. 
Keep refrigerated.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Pickle.name: Red Algae Pickle
preparation_steps: Wash Kappaphycus thoroughly and chop into small pieces.
Prepare brine with vinegar, garlic, and salt.
Mix seaweed pieces into brine.
Pack into sterilized jars.
Ferment 24-48 hours at room temperature.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Pickle.contraindications: High acidity & salt  avoid for gastric ulcers or hypertension.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Salt Subs.name: Seaweed Salt Substitute
preparation_steps: Grind Kappaphycus powder into fine consistency.
Sieve to remove large particles.
Blend with dried herbs if used.
Pack into airtight containers.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Salt Subs.contraindications: Still contains natural sodium, not sodium-free.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Salt Subs.recommended_dose: Use as normal salt replacement (?5 g/day)

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Hydration.name: Seaweed Hydration Spray
preparation_steps: Filter Kappaphycus extract to remove solids.
Mix extract with distilled water at room temperature.
Homogenize gently for 5-10 minutes.
Adjust pH to 5.5-6 using citric acid if necessary.
Fill into sterilized spray bottles.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Seaweed Hydration.contraindications:
External use only. 
Avoid eyes. 
Maintain sterile storage.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappa Herbal Toot.name: Kappa Herbal Toothpaste
preparation_steps: Melt coconut oil at 30°C.
Add Kappaphycus powder gradually while stirring.
Mix until uniform paste forms.
Add mint extract and blend.
Pack into sterilized toothpaste tubes.
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Kappa Herbal Toot.contraindications:
Do not swallow large quantities. 
Discontinue if irritation occurs.

Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Facial.name: Red Algae Facial Cleanser
preparation_steps: Mix Kappaphycus extract with glycerin.
Add surfactant solution slowly under gentle stirring.
Homogenize for 5 minutes.
Adjust pH to 5-6.
Fill into sterilized bottles.
critical_control_points: Avoid foaming too much during mixing
Kappaphycus Alvarezii||Red Algae Facial.contraindications:
